summary: theta-kit postinstall fetches and executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com paste
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook (`postinstall: node index.js`) runs lib/index.js, which decodes a base64-obfuscated URL stored under the misleading name `DEV_API_KEY` (decodes to `https://jsonkeeper.com/b/2P5FA`), HTTP GETs the paste, extracts the `cookie` field from the JSON response, and pipes those bytes into the stdin of a detached `node` child process with stdio suppressed (`spawn('node', [], {detached:true, stdio:['pipe','ignore','ignore']})`). This executes attacker-controlled JavaScript on the installer's machine at install time. The destination is a mutable anonymous paste host with no relationship to any legitimate publisher; the obfuscation (base64 literal + misleading variable name) demonstrates intent-to-evade. The package ships no real functionality — bootstrap.js, bundle.js, and publish.js are 0-byte stubs, and a console message (`Do not require('theta') directly...`) acts as a cover story impersonating a component-library packaging pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
